author: doll-agent
content: Translated OpenBLAS dgemm_kernel_4x8_haswell.S micro-kernel + INIT4x8 + KERNEL4x8 + SAVE4x8 macros to pure Rust (std::arch::x86_64 intrinsics). Source: OpenMathLib/OpenBLAS develop branch, BSD-3 licensed. Register tile is 4 rows × 8 cols, with BROADCASTKERNEL semantics each ymm holds one row's 4 contiguous columns — perfect for our row-major C output, so we drop the column-major transpose in SAVE and store rows directly via vmovups. Per K iter: 2 loads (B row, 8 doubles), 4 broadcasts (A column), 8 FMAs = 64 FLOPs. Prefetch A_PR1=512B, B_PR1=160B, B_PR1+64 (matches OpenBLAS line 859,865-867). Skip alpha mul when alpha==1.0. Plumbed via outer Goto 5-loop with MC=64, KC=256, NC=1024 + rayon-parallel IC chunks (PARALLEL_MIN_DIM=256). 19 unit tests + 12 property tests + 21 oracle tests pass. Single-thread perf is ~20% behind faer's private-gemm-x86 (more outer-loop tuning needed); rayon-parallel branch beats faer's broken Par::rayon at N>=256 (520us vs 2059us). Net effect on matmul speed: 50x50 was 10.6x slower than NumPy, now 1.3x; 100x100 was 2.1x, now 1.6x; 10x10 was 1.9x faster, now 6x faster (small-N naive ikj win). Mid-N gap (96-256) is single-threaded 4x8 vs NumPy's 28-thread MKL — only multithreading closes that. Path forward: tighter outer loop + smaller MC for parallel at smaller N, or link real OpenBLAS.
